name: Mobile Toilet
description:
A mobile toilet is the unit you order when sanitation is needed at a spot a delivery truck can barely reach — a tower base off the main road, a survey camp on open ground, or a remote stretch of pipeline with no pad and no plumbing. It is one self-contained sanitation unit, built to be lifted in, used, and lifted out again.

What Is a Mobile Toilet?

A mobile toilet is a compact, self-contained single sanitation unit built to be lifted and transported — craned, forklifted, or loaded onto a truck — then set down on level ground wherever it is needed and moved on when the work ends. The name describes how the unit travels, not only that it is portable.

That mobility-by-transport is the whole point of this unit, and it shapes everything from the size to the price. It is one format within SAMAN's wider portable toilet range, and the last section on this page helps you place it against the two units it is most often confused with.

What Makes It "Mobile" — Crane, Forklift and Truck Handling

A mobile toilet craned off a flatbed — the lifting lugs let it drop into spots a truck can't reach.

The mobile toilet is a single-occupant unit measuring 6 × 6 × 7 ft, built from a powder-coated steel structure with a finished wood interior — small and self-contained by design, because the job is to move it cleanly into and out of an awkward location. The base is built to be handled: it takes a forklift under the frame or slings from a crane hook, so it can be lifted off a flatbed and placed exactly where access allows. A crane- or truck-movable toilet of this size needs no foundation and no civil work — it sits on any reasonably level, firm patch of ground and is ready to use once water is connected. When the location is done with it, the same unit lifts back onto the vehicle and travels to the next point.

Where a Mobile Toilet Works Best — Remote and Hard-Access Sites

A mobile toilet serving a remote telecom tower base where no fixed toilet could be installed.

This unit earns its place where a fixed toilet can't be installed and a delivery crew can't simply roll a cabin off a truck. Typical deployments: telecom tower bases and substations reached by rough track, geological or survey camps on open land, remote points along a highway or pipeline corridor, disaster-relief and flood camps, and the back-of-house service area of an outdoor event set up on uneven ground.

A forklift slots into the base pockets to place the mobile toilet exactly where access allows.

For water at these off-grid spots, the unit takes an overhead tank your team tops up or a built-in septic tank where there is no connection at all, while a wall vent and roof vent keep the interior from turning stale in summer. The pan is supplied as either an Indian squat or a Western seat, confirmed before dispatch to suit who will use it.

Mobile Toilet Price — ₹80,000 and What Moves It

A SAMAN mobile toilet is priced at ₹80,000 (regular ₹90,000), inclusive of all taxes, for the standard 6 × 6 × 7 ft single unit. That covers the steel structure, the finished wood interior, the fitted pan, the water inlet, wall-and-roof ventilation, the lockable door, and the powder-coated exterior in your choice of colour.

Same-day off-load: the unit is levelled and the water inlet connected, ready to use without civil work.

What moves the mobile toilet price from there: the pan type you choose, a built-in septic-tank build for fully off-grid locations, and a custom exterior colour to match site or event branding. The unit is delivered across India from SAMAN's own facilities and, on an accessible site, off-loaded and levelled the same day.

Mobile Toilet or Another SAMAN Toilet Unit? Pick by How It Travels

A mobile toilet in service along a pipeline corridor — lifted out and moved on once that stretch is done.

Three SAMAN units relocate, and the right one comes down to how the toilet has to travel. Choose this mobile toilet when the real problem is access — the unit has to be craned or trucked into one remote or difficult spot, used, then recovered. If instead you want a roomier finished cabin to set down and use almost anywhere — an event ground, an exhibition, a new site before plumbing arrives — then a roomier cabin you place and use anywhere is the better fit. And if the toilet has to follow an advancing work front, repositioning spot to spot as one project moves along a road or pipeline, look at a unit built to move with the work instead.

Frequently Asked Questions

What are mobile toilets called?

They go by several names — mobile toilet, mobile toilet unit, portable sanitation unit, or simply a movable toilet. At SAMAN the term "mobile toilet" refers specifically to this compact single unit made to be craned or trucked into position, as distinct from a full toilet cabin.

How does a mobile toilet work?

It is a complete sanitation unit you place on level ground with no civil work. Water comes from an overhead tank or a connected line, waste collects in a septic or holding tank, and wall-and-roof vents keep air moving. When the location no longer needs it, the unit is lifted onto a vehicle and moved on.

How much does a mobile toilet cost?

The standard 6 × 6 × 7 ft unit is ₹80,000 (regular ₹90,000), inclusive of all taxes, and it is sold as a unit you own. The final figure depends on the pan type, whether you add a septic tank, and any custom colour.

Mobile toilet or mobile toilet cabin — which do I need?

Choose the mobile toilet when access is the challenge and the unit has to be craned or trucked into a hard-to-reach spot. Choose the larger cabin format when you want a roomier, single- or two-cubicle unit to place and use at more accessible locations. Same product family — the deciding factor is access difficulty and how much room you need.

Get a Quote

Tell us the location, the access situation, the pan type, and your water arrangement, and SAMAN will send a mobile toilet quote with delivery timing. Call +91 80886 85440 or send your requirement on WhatsApp at +91 97089 89937 for a same-day response.
short_description:
Mobile Toilet — compact 6×6×7 ft single unit, crane- or truck-movable
A self-contained sanitation unit built to be lifted into remote, hard-access sites and moved on when the work ends.
Size / Capacity
6×6×7 ft · 1 person
Frame / Panel
MS steel · steel & wood
Price / Delivery
₹80,000 · Pan-India
FloorLoad / Relocations
Single WC · crane/truck
Inclusive of all taxes
From ₹80,000
